Yes, just having students “use AI to study” hurts learning (a helpful assistant is not a tutor), but using AI prompted to act like a tutor, especially with teacher support, seems to have large positive effects on learning in randomized trials. https://t.co/0HtjGC8eU0 https://t.co/tjkygnFCGc

LLMs don't think. You do.

So before I ask AI to do anything serious, I run this prompt first.

3x more upvoted on r/ChatGPT than any other prompt type. Here's my version:

-------------------------
THINKING PROTOCOL
-------------------------

You are a senior expert in [your

How to deploy:

1. Copy the full prompt above
2. Replace [your domain] with your real context (legal, marketing, code review, etc.)
3. Paste your actual request at the bottom and let it interview you first

The output difference is jarring once you start using this before every
